sentiment.label: risk_off
sentiment.reason_it: Il sentiment di mercato è decisamente risk-off oggi, con un aumento significativo del VIX (+11.31% a 26.78) e un calo generalizzato degli asset rischiosi. L'S&P 500 perde l'1.51% a 6506.48, mentre il Nasdaq e lo STOXX 600 registrano perdite simili. Il DXY si rafforza (+0.13% a 99.78), mentre l'EUR/USD scende (-0.38% a 1.15). Il prezzo del petrolio (Brent) crolla del 4.14% a $107.54, e l'oro perde il 5.57% a $4315.90. Questo quadro suggerisce una forte avversione al rischio, probabilmente dovuta alle crescenti tensioni geopolitiche nel Medio Oriente.
sentiment.reason_en: The market sentiment is strongly risk-off today, with a significant increase in VIX (+11.31% to 26.78) and a generalized decline in risky assets. The S&P 500 drops 1.51% to 6506.48, while the Nasdaq and STOXX 600 post similar losses. The DXY strengthens (+0.13% to 99.78), while the EUR/USD falls (-0.38% to 1.15). Oil prices (Brent) plummet 4.14% to $107.54, and gold loses 5.57% to $4315.90. This scenario suggests a strong risk aversion, likely due to rising geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

market_impact_summary.it: Il mercato azionario è in forte calo, con l'S&P 500 che perde l'1.51% e il VIX che sale del 11.31%. Il dollaro si rafforza (+0.13% DXY), mentre l'euro scende (-0.38% EUR/USD). Il prezzo del petrolio crolla del 4.14% (Brent), e l'oro perde il 5.57%. I tassi di interesse salgono, con il US 10Y Yield che raggiunge il 4.39% (+2.57%).
market_impact_summary.en: The stock market is sharply down, with the S&P 500 losing 1.51% and VIX surging 11.31%. The dollar strengthens (+0.13% DXY), while the euro falls (-0.38% EUR/USD). Oil prices plummet 4.14% (Brent), and gold drops 5.57%. Interest rates rise, with the US 10Y Yield reaching 4.39% (+2.57%).

audio_script_en:
{'INTRODUCTION': "Welcome to your daily morning market briefing. Good morning and welcome to today's financial update.", 'SENTIMENT': 'Global sentiment is risk-off today, as we see a broad sell-off across equities and safe-havens like bonds and gold being bought. Key data points include the US Non-Farm Payrolls, which came in at -92K, and the Tasso Disoccupazione USA, which rose to 4.4%.', 'TRADITIONAL MARKETS': 'In traditional markets, equities are under pressure. The S&P 500 is down 1.51% to 6506.48, and the STOXX 600 is down 1.77% to 56.70. The NIKKEI and HANG SENG indices in Asia also saw significant declines, down 3.55% and 3.69% respectively. In currencies, the EUR/USD is down 0.38% to 1.15, and the Dollar Index (DXY) is up 0.13% to 99.78. Commodities are also feeling the heat, with GOLD down 5.57% to $4315.90/oz, and BRENT crude down 4.14% to $107.54. Bond yields are rising, with the US 10Y Yield up 2.57% to 4.39%, and the BTP 10Y up 5.00% to 3.96%.', 'GEOPOLITICS_MACRO': "Geopolitics and macroeconomics are closely intertwined today, with rising tensions in the Middle East dominating headlines. The ongoing conflict has raised concerns about inflation and economic stability. The recent US Fed Funds rate remains steady at 3.64%, but with PCE Core YoY at 3.1%, and Core CPI YoY USA at 2.7%, the macroeconomic landscape remains challenging. The ECB's recent rate hike to 2.00% also highlights the ongoing battle against inflation. As we navigate these uncertain times, market participants will be closely watching any developments on the geopolitical front and their potential impact on economic data."}

Moving on to the digital assets space, let's take a closer look at the current state of the cryptocurrency market. 

The Bitcoin price is currently trading at $68,706, down 0.52% from the previous day. From a technical analysis perspective, we can see that the recent inflows into Bitcoin have been quite positive, but the current price action suggests a bit of consolidation. The flows into Bitcoin have been steady, but we need to see a more significant breakout to confirm a trend.

Let's pivot to the cryptocurrency markets and examine some of the top altcoins. Ethereum is currently trading at $2,060, down 2.25% from the previous day, while Solana is at $86.94, down 1.67%. Binance Coin is also down 0.45% at $629.92. Ethereum's recent performance has been closely watched, especially with the upcoming upgrades to its network. Solana and Binance Coin have also been making significant strides in their respective ecosystems.

In terms of market sentiment, the Fear & Greed Index is currently at 8, indicating extreme fear. This is often a sign that investors are overly pessimistic, which can lead to a buying opportunity. The correlation between cryptocurrencies and other assets, such as stocks and gold, has been a topic of interest lately. The divergence between Bitcoin and gold reflects the split between retail and central banks, with some analysts suggesting that this could be a sign of a changing market landscape.

Overall, the cryptocurrency market continues to be highly volatile, but there are still opportunities for growth and investment. As always, it's essential to do your own research and consider your own risk tolerance before making any investment decisions.

Thanks for tuning in. We'll be back tomorrow with another market update. Until then, stay informed and stay ahead. A quick look at what's on the radar for tomorrow and we'll wrap up here. Have a great day and we'll catch you soon.

articles.title: FirstFT: Stocks fall on Trump’s Iran ultimatum

articles.summary_it: Le tensioni tra Iran e Trump stanno influenzando negativamente i mercati, con un aumento della volatilità e un calo degli asset rischiosi.
articles.summary_en: Tensions between Iran and Trump are negatively impacting markets, with increased volatility and a decline in risky assets.

articles.title: How the Iran war could derail the AI boom
articles.snippet: The entire chip supply chain depends on energy and chemical imports from the Middle East
articles.title_it: La guerra in Iran potrebbe fermare il boom dell'AI
articles.title_en: Iran war could derail AI boom
articles.summary_it: La guerra in Iran potrebbe avere un impatto negativo sul boom dell'intelligenza artificiale, a causa della dipendenza dalle importazioni di energia e prodotti chimici dal Medio Oriente.
articles.summary_en: The war in Iran could have a negative impact on the AI boom, due to dependence on energy and chemical imports from the Middle East.

articles.title: The realities of economic security
articles.snippet: The Peterson Institute for International Economics presents a high-level analytical discussion of the realities of economic security, examining real-world trade-offs and vulnerabilities in today's geopolitically fractured world. Anjali Bhatt, host of PIIE Insider Live, moderates a candid discussion with PIIE experts: Warwick McKibbin, who presents global macroeconomic scenarios of the impact of the Middle East conflict; Cullen Hendrix, who discusses vulnerabilities of the global economy to the disrupted supply of commodities including food, fuel, and industrial inputs; and Jacob Funk…
articles.summary_it: Il Peterson Institute for International Economics presenterà una discussione di alto livello sulla sicurezza economica e sui compromessi nel mondo geopoliticamente fratturato.
articles.summary_en: The Peterson Institute for International Economics will present a high-level discussion on economic security and trade-offs in today's geopolitically fractured world.

articles.title: Amid War, Yemen’s New Government Faces Challenges at Home and Abroad
articles.snippet: Yemen has a new government, formed after elements of the old one clashed on the battlefield. In this Q&A, Crisis Group expert Ahmed Nagi looks at its makeup and its prospects against the backdrop of Middle East war that may draw in the Houthi rebels.

articles.title: Where is the Israel-Hizbollah War Going?
articles.snippet: Israel and Hizbollah are once again fighting head to head, with disastrous repercussions for Lebanon. In this Q&A, Crisis Group experts Heiko Wimmen, David Wood and Max Rodenbeck consider what might come next.

articles.title: Trump’s Iran Uranium Dilemma Raises Stakes for Oil Markets
articles.snippet: Trump faces a simple but difficult choice: Iran’s enriched uranium still needs to be secured, and airstrikes haven’t accomplished that. The only clear way to control it would likely require U.S. troops on the ground—an option that risks widening the conflict and raising the stakes significantly. That uncertainty has been pouring over into global energy markets. Oil prices have pulled back from recent highs but remain elevated as the Middle East conflict shows no signs of abating. Europe remains largely non-committal towards using…

articles.title: Nuclear in the Spotlight amid Oil, Gas Crunch
articles.snippet: Nuclear energy has been making a comeback recently as one of the low-carbon baseload generation options. This comeback is set to accelerate amid the Middle Eastern crisis and the oil and gas squeeze it is causing. The only problem is that nuclear capacity takes a while to build. “It was a strategic mistake for Europe to turn its back on a reliable, affordable source of low-emission power,” European Commission president Ursula von der Leyen admitted earlier this month at a nuclear energy summit in France. Von der Leyen herself had voted…

articles.title: Oil Markets Brace for Volatility as Trump’s Iran Deadline Fuels Escalation Fears
articles.snippet: Oil prices opened the week elevated but stable in early Asian trading on Monday, holding near multi-year highs after another weekend of escalating tensions between the United States and Iran. At the time of writing, WTI crude was trading at $98.61, up 0.39%, while Brent crude had inched down 0.24% to trade at $111.90. These muted price moves mask an increasingly uncertain environment, with markets bracing for a very volatile session. Late on Saturday, President Trump issued a stark ultimatum, warning that the United States would strike Iran’s…
